---
title: "3.3 Anatomy of a Class"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### A class is a blueprint for objects

In Java, a <span class="term">class</span> defines what objects of that type store and what they can do.

The core parts are:

::: {.tight-list}
- instance variables: object data
- constructors: object initialization
- methods: object behaviors
:::

## Parts of a Class

### Read the class body by role

::: {.code-scroll .compact}
```java
public class Person
{
    private String name;
    private String email;
    private String phoneNumber;

    public Person(String initialName, String initialEmail, String initialPhoneNumber)
    {
        name = initialName;
        email = initialEmail;
        phoneNumber = initialPhoneNumber;
    }

    public void print()
    {
        System.out.println(name);
        System.out.println(email);
        System.out.println(phoneNumber);
    }
}
```
:::

## Designing a Class

### Choose data based on the problem

The same real-world object can be modeled differently in different programs.

::: {.tight-list}
- address book: `name`, `email`, `phoneNumber`
- school system: `studentId`, `gradeLevel`, `gpa`
- game: `health`, `location`, `score`
:::

Good design stores the information the program actually needs.

## Data Encapsulation

### Keep instance variables private

<span class="term">Data encapsulation</span> (数据封装) means protecting an object's internal state.

::: {.compare-grid}
::: {.soft-box}
**`private` instance variables**

- accessible inside the class
- protected from direct outside changes
- standard AP CSA class design
:::
::: {.soft-box}
**`public` constructors and methods**

- used by other classes
- form the public interface
- can validate or control changes
:::
:::

## Instance Variables

### Each object has its own data

![Person class and separate object data](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-3-anatomy-of-a-class/PersonClassWithCookies.png){fig-align="center" width="62%"}

Two `Person` objects can have different names and emails because each object has its own copy of the instance variables.

## Identify Task

### Find the instance variables

In this class, which lines declare instance variables?

```java
public class Pet
{
    private String name;
    private int age;

    public Pet(String petName, int petAge)
    {
        name = petName;
        age = petAge;
    }
}
```

Answer: `private String name;` and `private int age;`.

## Instance Methods

### Methods act on one specific object

```java
public void print()
{
    System.out.println(name);
    System.out.println(email);
    System.out.println(phoneNumber);
}
```

An instance method is called on an object:

```java
p1.print();
```

It can use that object's instance variables directly.

## Code Task

### Add a print method

Add a `print` method to the class.

```java
public class Person
{
    private String name;

    public Person(String initialName)
    {
        name = initialName;
    }

    public void print()
    {
        System.out.println(name);
    }
}
```

Then call it with `new Person("Ada").print();`.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge {.fit-small .image-fit}

### Virtual Pet Class

![Virtual pet prompt](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-3-anatomy-of-a-class/virtualpet.jpg){fig-align="center" width="28%"}

Starter design:

```java
public class VirtualPet
{
    private String name;
    private int hunger;

    public VirtualPet(String petName)
    {
        name = petName;
        hunger = 5;
    }
}
```

Add at least one method that changes or prints the pet's state.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- identify the roles of instance variables, constructors, and methods
- explain why instance variables are usually `private`
- explain that each object has its own copy of instance data
- distinguish class design from object creation
- add a simple instance method that uses instance variables
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
